title: Identify Quadrilaterals — Square, Rectangle, Rhombus, Parallelogram, Trapezoid
essential_question: How can you identify special types of quadrilaterals by their attributes?

pretest.question: Which figure is a quadrilateral?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Quadrilaterals have exactly 4 sides.

feedback.correct: Yes! A square is a 4-sided polygon, which is a quadrilateral.
feedback.incorrect: The answer is B. A square has 4 sides, so it is a quadrilateral.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A square is a 4-sided polygon, which is a quadrilateral.

pretest.question: Which quadrilateral has 4 equal sides but does NOT need to have 4 right angles?

A.note: A square always has 4 right angles too

B.note: A rectangle has 4 right angles but not all equal sides

D.note: Trapezoids do not need 4 equal sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Find the shape with all sides equal but corners that may not be square.

feedback.correct: Yes! A rhombus has 4 equal sides — its corners may not all be right angles.
feedback.incorrect: A rhombus has 4 equal sides but does not need 4 right angles.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A rhombus has 4 equal sides — its corners may not all be right angles.

pretest.question: Which is a TRAPEZOID?
choices: A) A quadrilateral with NO pairs of parallel sides
B) A quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides
C) A quadrilateral with 4 right angles

A.note: Trapezoids have at least one pair of parallel sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Trapezoid: 4-sided with at least one pair of parallel sides.

hints: Trapezoids have parallel sides — at least one pair.

feedback.correct: Yes! A trapezoid is a quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides.
feedback.incorrect: A trapezoid is a 4-sided polygon with at least one pair of parallel sides.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A trapezoid is a quadrilateral with at least one pair of parallel sides.

pretest.question: How many pairs of parallel sides does a parallelogram have?

1.note: A parallelogram has BOTH pairs of opposite sides parallel

0.note: A parallelogram has parallel sides — that is in the name

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: The word 'parallelogram' starts with 'parallel'. How many pairs?

feedback.correct: Right! A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides.
feedback.incorrect: A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides — both pairs of opposite sides are parallel.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides.

pretest.question: Which is a RECTANGLE that is NOT a square?
choices: A) A quadrilateral with 4 sides all 5 cm long and 4 right angles
B) A quadrilateral with 2 sides 6 cm and 2 sides 4 cm and 4 right angles
C) A quadrilateral with 4 equal sides and no right angles

A.note: All sides equal AND 4 right angles = a SQUARE

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Rectangle (not square): 2 long sides + 2 short sides + 4 right angles.

hints: A rectangle has 4 right angles but 2 long and 2 short sides — not a square.

feedback.correct: Yes! 6 cm and 4 cm sides with 4 right angles — that is a rectangle (not a square).
feedback.incorrect: A non-square rectangle has 2 long sides and 2 short sides plus 4 right angles. Choice B fits.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! 6 cm and 4 cm sides with 4 right angles — that is a rectangle (not a square).

learn.title: Quadrilateral — The 4-Sided Family
learn.content: A QUADRILATERAL is any polygon with 4 sides and 4 angles. Squares, rectangles, rhombuses, parallelograms, and trapezoids are all quadrilaterals. The word 'quadrilateral' just means '4-sided polygon' — it does not say anything special about the sides or angles.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Square — All Equal AND All Right
learn.content: A SQUARE is a special quadrilateral with TWO attributes at once: (1) all 4 sides are equal, AND (2) all 4 angles are right angles. Examples: chessboard squares, floor tiles, sticky notes that are perfectly square. If a shape has 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles, it must be a square.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Rectangle — 4 Right Angles
learn.content: A RECTANGLE is a quadrilateral with 4 right angles. Its OPPOSITE sides are equal (top = bottom, left = right), but the long sides do not have to match the short sides. Examples: a door, a book cover, a phone screen. Note: every square IS also a rectangle (because squares have 4 right angles too).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Rhombus — All 4 Sides Equal
learn.content: A RHOMBUS is a quadrilateral with all 4 sides equal in length. Its angles do not have to be right angles. A rhombus often looks like a tilted, diamond-shaped square. Note: every square IS also a rhombus (because squares have 4 equal sides too).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Parallel Sides
learn.content: Two sides are PARALLEL if they go in the same direction and never cross — like two railroad tracks. PARALLEL SIDES are sides of a polygon that are parallel to each other. We use this idea to define more quadrilaterals: parallelograms and trapezoids.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Parallelogram — 2 Pairs of Parallel Sides
learn.content: A PARALLELOGRAM is a quadrilateral with 2 pairs of parallel sides — both pairs of opposite sides are parallel. Squares, rectangles, and rhombuses are ALL parallelograms (each has 2 pairs of parallel sides). The opposite sides of a parallelogram are also equal in length.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Trapezoid — At Least 1 Pair of Parallel Sides
learn.content: A TRAPEZOID is a quadrilateral with AT LEAST ONE pair of parallel sides. Some trapezoids have only one pair of parallel sides (the other two sides slant in different directions). Note: in some textbooks, parallelograms count as special trapezoids.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Lesson Summary
learn.content: Today you learned the quadrilateral family: (1) QUADRILATERAL = any 4-sided polygon. (2) SQUARE = 4 equal sides + 4 right angles. (3) RECTANGLE = 4 right angles. (4) RHOMBUS = 4 equal sides. (5) PARALLELOGRAM = 2 pairs of parallel sides. (6) TRAPEZOID = at least 1 pair of parallel sides. A square is a special rectangle, rhombus, AND parallelogram!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

try.question: Which describes a square?
choices: B) 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles
solution_steps: Step 1: A square needs 4 equal sides.
Step 2: A square also needs 4 right angles.
Step 3: BOTH attributes together define a square.

C.note: That's a rectangle (not necessarily a square)

D.note: That's a rhombus (not necessarily a square)

feedback.correct: Right! A square has 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles.
feedback.incorrect: A square has BOTH 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles — both attributes together.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: A square has TWO attributes at once.
try.feedback_correct: Right! A square has 4 equal sides AND 4 right angles.

try.question: Maya draws a quadrilateral with 4 right angles. Sides: 8 cm, 5 cm, 8 cm, 5 cm. What is it?
solution_steps: Step 1: Sides are 8 and 5 — not all equal.
Step 2: But it has 4 right angles.
Step 3: 4 right angles + opposite sides equal but not all equal = rectangle (not square).

D.note: Trapezoids do not require 4 right angles

feedback.correct: Right! 4 right angles with two long and two short sides = rectangle (not a square).
feedback.incorrect: Maya's shape has 4 right angles but sides are not all equal — it is a rectangle (not a square).

verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Rectangle: 4 right angles, opposite sides equal.

hints: 4 right angles, but are all sides equal?
try.feedback_correct: Right! 4 right angles with two long and two short sides = rectangle (not a square).

try.question: How many pairs of parallel sides does a rectangle have?
solution_steps: Step 1: A rectangle has 2 long sides and 2 short sides.
Step 2: The long sides are parallel to each other.
Step 3: The short sides are also parallel to each other.
Step 4: That's 2 pairs.

1.note: Both pairs of opposite sides are parallel — 2 pairs

4.note: Don't count individual sides — count PAIRS

feedback.correct: Right! A rectangle has 2 pairs of parallel sides.
feedback.incorrect: A rectangle has 2 pairs of parallel sides — top with bottom, left with right.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Both pairs of opposite sides are parallel.
try.feedback_correct: Right! A rectangle has 2 pairs of parallel sides.

try.question: Sara draws a quadrilateral with all 4 sides 6 cm long, but the angles are NOT right angles. What is it?
solution_steps: Step 1: All 4 sides are equal (6 cm).
Step 2: No right angles.
Step 3: 4 equal sides + no right angles = rhombus.

feedback.correct: Right! 4 equal sides and no right angles = rhombus.
feedback.incorrect: All 4 sides equal but no right angles = rhombus.

practice_r3.question: Sara says, 'My quadrilateral has all 4 sides equal AND 4 right angles.' How many shape names fit Sara's quadrilateral? (Square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram)
solution_steps: Step 1: Square — yes (4 equal sides + 4 right angles).
Step 2: Rectangle — yes (4 right angles).
Step 3: Rhombus — yes (4 equal sides).
Step 4: Parallelogram — yes (2 pairs of parallel sides).
Step 5: All 4 names fit.

A.note: It also fits rectangle, rhombus, and parallelogram

B.note: It also fits rhombus and parallelogram

feedback.correct: Right! A square fits ALL 4 names — square, rectangle, rhombus, and parallelogram.
feedback.incorrect: All 4 names fit. A square is a rectangle (right angles), a rhombus (equal sides), AND a parallelogram (parallel sides).

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What two attributes does Sara's shape have? Read 2: Which other shape names have those attributes? Read 3: Count all that fit.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals

practice_r3.question: Which shape has the FEWEST attributes (most general definition)?
solution_steps: Step 1: Quadrilateral just means 4 sides — no other rules.
Step 2: Square needs 4 sides + 4 equal sides + 4 right angles.
Step 3: Rectangle needs 4 sides + 4 right angles.
Step 4: Rhombus needs 4 sides + 4 equal sides.
Step 5: Quadrilateral has the fewest rules.

B.note: Rectangle has more rules than quadrilateral

D.note: Rhombus has more rules than quadrilateral

feedback.correct: Right! Quadrilateral has the fewest rules — just 4 sides. The others all add more rules.
feedback.incorrect: Quadrilateral has the simplest definition: just 4 sides. Other shapes add more attributes.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: How many rules does each shape have? Read 2: Which has the fewest? Read 3: That shape covers the most others.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Which name has the simplest definition?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! Quadrilateral has the fewest rules — just 4 sides. The others all add more rules.

practice_r3.question: Look at this list: square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram, trapezoid. How many of these are ALWAYS parallelograms? (A square IS a parallelogram.)
solution_steps: Step 1: Square — has 2 pairs of parallel sides. YES.
Step 2: Rectangle — has 2 pairs of parallel sides. YES.
Step 3: Rhombus — has 2 pairs of parallel sides. YES.
Step 4: Parallelogram — by definition. YES.
Step 5: Trapezoid — only 1 pair of parallel sides (in 3rd grade). NO.
Step 6: 4 of the 5 are parallelograms.

5.note: Trapezoid has only 1 pair of parallel sides — not a parallelogram

feedback.correct: Right! Square, rectangle, rhombus, and parallelogram all have 2 pairs of parallel sides. Trapezoid only has 1.
feedback.incorrect: 4 are parallelograms (square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram). Trapezoid is NOT a parallelogram.

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What is the list? Read 2: What does parallelogram require? Read 3: How many of the list have 2 pairs of parallel sides?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: 4 of 5 always have 2 pairs of parallel sides.

hints: Which one has only 1 pair of parallel sides?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! Square, rectangle, rhombus, and parallelogram all have 2 pairs of parallel sides. Trapezoid only has 1.

practice_r3.question: Ben says, 'I drew a quadrilateral that is a rhombus AND a rectangle.' Which shape did Ben draw?
choices: D) Impossible — no shape is both
solution_steps: Step 1: Rhombus needs 4 equal sides.
Step 2: Rectangle needs 4 right angles.
Step 3: 4 equal sides + 4 right angles = SQUARE.
Step 4: A square is the only shape that is both a rhombus and a rectangle.

A.note: Trapezoids do not need both attributes

B.note: A parallelogram that is also a rhombus AND rectangle must be a square

D.note: Squares are both rhombuses and rectangles

feedback.correct: Right! A shape that is both a rhombus AND a rectangle must be a square.
feedback.incorrect: A square is the only shape that is both a rhombus (4 equal sides) AND a rectangle (4 right angles).

practice_r3.three_read_prompt: Read 1: What two definitions must Ben's shape match? Read 2: What shape has BOTH? Read 3: Which choice fits?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Combine the rules of a rhombus and a rectangle. What shape has both?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Right! A shape that is both a rhombus AND a rectangle must be a square.
